message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-021 in src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
What: build_baseline_artifact names the offending extra_input key when not canonicalizable.
Test(s): test_build_artifact_names_the_bad_extra_input
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

---

1	"""Determinism & reproducibility harness for the baseline (ADR-0001, METHODOLOGY.md §1, §7).
2	
3	A baseline is only a yardstick if the *same inputs reproduce the same baseline*. This module
4	makes that contract concrete and engine-level: it content-hashes inputs, derives seeds
5	deterministically from those hashes, records component versions, and stamps all of it into a
6	:class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineArtifact`'s ``determinism`` record — so a
7	hostile reviewer can confirm a baseline was produced exactly as claimed.
8	
9	It is dependency-free and pure. The heavy, *choice-bearing* builders (which embedding model,
10	which clustering algorithm, which graph construction) encode dataset/study decisions and are
11	supplied by a study's adapter as :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`
12	implementations — not prescribed by the engine (ADR-0002). This harness records *how* such a
13	builder ran so the result is reproducible regardless of which builder it was.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import hashlib
19	import json
20	import sys
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	from typing import Any, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine import __version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION
25	
26	# The canonical hashing primitives moved to ``assay_engine._canonical`` (see note below); these
27	# imports re-export them at their historical home so existing call sites keep working.
28	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_plain as _plain  # noqa: F401
29	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_bytes as hash_bytes  # noqa: F401  (re-export)
30	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_text as hash_text  # noqa: F401  (re-export, historical home)
31	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value as hash_value  # re-export (baseline public API)
32	from assay_engine._canonical import keytag as _keytag  # noqa: F401
33	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
34	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
35	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
36	
37	# The canonical hashing primitives (``_plain``/``_keytag``/``hash_bytes``/``hash_text``/
38	# ``hash_value``) live in ``assay_engine._canonical`` so they sit below both ``baseline`` and
39	# ``methodology`` in the import graph (audit #B1/#B2/#B5 originally landed here; hoisted so
40	# pre-registration can content-bind a hypothesis with the *same* type-faithful digest). They are
41	# re-exported above for back-compat.
42	
43	
44	def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
45	    """Deterministic content hash of a corpus (order-independent across units/relations).
46	
47	    The fingerprint is SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of the corpus payload. The JSON is
48	    streamed into the hash via ``JSONEncoder.iterencode`` rather than materialized as one giant
49	    string (pass 3, #F-016): for a large corpus this avoids holding the full serialized payload
50	    in RAM on top of the row dicts at hash time. ``iterencode`` with the same options yields the
51	    identical byte stream ``json.dumps`` would, so the fingerprint is byte-for-byte unchanged.
52	    """
53	    units = [
54	        {"id": u.unit_id, "text": u.text, "attrs": _plain(u.attributes)}
55	        for u in sorted(corpus.units, key=lambda u: u.unit_id)
56	    ]
57	    relations = [
58	        {"s": r.source_id, "t": r.target_id, "k": r.kind, "attrs": _plain(r.attributes)}
59	        for r in sorted(corpus.relations, key=lambda r: (r.source_id, r.target_id, r.kind))
60	    ]
61	    payload = {"units": units, "relations": relations, "metadata": _plain(corpus.metadata)}
62	    h = hashlib.sha256()
63	    encoder = json.JSONEncoder(sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False)
64	    for chunk in encoder.iterencode(payload):
65	        h.update(chunk.encode("utf-8"))
66	    return h.hexdigest()
67	
68	
69	def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
70	    """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
71	
72	    Derived from a hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible without a
73	    hard-coded magic number, and distinct inputs get distinct seeds.
74	    """
75	    digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
76	    return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)
77	
78	
79	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
80	class DeterminismRecord:
81	    """The reproducibility provenance stamped onto a baseline (ADR-0001)."""
82	
83	    seed: int
84	    input_hashes: Mapping[str, str]
85	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str]
86	
87	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
88	        object.__setattr__(self, "input_hashes", freeze_mapping(self.input_hashes))
89	        object.__setattr__(self, "component_versions", freeze_mapping(self.component_versions))
90	
91	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
92	        return {
93	            "seed": self.seed,
94	            "input_hashes": dict(self.input_hashes),
95	            "component_versions": dict(self.component_versions),
96	        }
97	
98	
99	def build_baseline_artifact(
100	    corpus: Corpus,
101	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
102	    *,
103	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
104	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
105	    seed: int | None = None,
106	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
107	    trust_corpus_hash: bool = False,
108	) -> BaselineArtifact:
109	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
110	
111	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
112	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
113	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
114	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
115	    so it is reproducible.
116	
117	    ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed :func:`corpus_fingerprint` (#119). By
118	    default it is **verified** against the corpus and a mismatch raises ``ValueError`` — a
119	    stale/typo'd hash would otherwise produce a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived
120	    seed describe a *different* corpus than the one passed, silently defeating the reproducibility
121	    guarantee this harness exists to provide (#155). Pass ``trust_corpus_hash=True`` to skip the
122	    recompute (the #119 optimization) only when the caller computed the hash from this exact
123	    corpus and accepts integrity responsibility.
124	    """
125	    if corpus_hash is None:
126	        corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
127	    elif not trust_corpus_hash:
128	        # Verify the supplied hash binds the corpus actually passed (default-safe, #155).
129	        actual = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
130	        if corpus_hash != actual:
131	            raise ValueError(
132	                f"corpus_hash {corpus_hash!r} does not match the corpus "
133	                f"(corpus_fingerprint={actual!r}) — the determinism record would bind the wrong "
134	                "data; pass trust_corpus_hash=True only if you accept integrity responsibility"
135	            )
136	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
137	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
138	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
139	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
140	        # Name the offending key when an extra input is not canonicalizable (#G-021): hash_value
141	        # raises a raw ValueError (non-finite) / TypeError (unreproducible repr) with no context,
142	        # so a rich config with one bad value gave an opaque error. Re-raise with the key.
143	        try:
144	            input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
145	        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
146	            raise type(exc)(f"extra_input {name!r} is not canonicalizable: {exc}") from exc
147	
148	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
149	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
150	
151	    if seed is None:
152	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
153	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
154	
155	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
156	    return BaselineArtifact(
157	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
158	    )
159

content.text: The fix is at lines 139-146. Now let me find the test.

100	    class Weird:
101	        __slots__ = ()
102

104	        hash_value({"k": Weird()})
105	
106	
107	def test_hash_value_rejects_non_finite_float():
108	    # issue #B4: NaN/inf must surface, not be encoded as JSON NaN/Infinity tokens
109	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
110	        hash_value(float("nan"))
111	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
112	        hash_value([1.0, float("inf")])
113	
114	
115	def test_build_artifact_reserves_corpus_input_key():
116	    # issue #B7: an extra input named "corpus" must not clobber the corpus fingerprint
117	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
118	        build_baseline_artifact(_corpus(), {}, extra_inputs={"corpus": "x"})
119	
120	
121	def test_build_artifact_records_full_determinism_and_is_reproducible():
122	    c = _corpus()
123	    a1 = build_baseline_artifact(c, {"sim": [[1.0]]}, component_versions={"builder": "demo@1"})
124	    a2 = build_baseline_artifact(c, {"sim": [[1.0]]}, component_versions={"builder": "demo@1"})
125	    assert a1.corpus_fingerprint == corpus_fingerprint(c)
126	    det = dict(a1.determinism)
127	    assert det["seed"] == dict(a2.determinism)["seed"]  # reproducible seed from same inputs
128	    assert det["input_hashes"]["corpus"] == corpus_fingerprint(c)
129	    assert det["component_versions"]["builder"] == "demo@1"
130	    assert "engine" in det["component_versions"] and "python" in det["component_versions"]
131	    assert hash(a1) is not None  # frozen + hashable (FrozenDict contents/determinism)
132	
133	
134	def test_build_artifact_names_the_bad_extra_input():
135	    # #G-021: a non-canonicalizable extra input must raise an error NAMING the offending key, not
136	    # an opaque raw ValueError/TypeError from hash_value with no context.
137	    with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError), match="extra_input 'cfg'"):
138	        build_baseline_artifact(_corpus(), {}, extra_inputs={"cfg": object()})
139	
140	
141	def test_build_artifact_extra_inputs_change_seed():
142	    c = _corpus()
143	    base = build_baseline_artifact(c, {}, extra_inputs={"cfg": {"alpha": 0.05}})
144	    other = build_baseline_artifact(c, {}, extra_inputs={"cfg": {"alpha": 0.01}})
145	    assert dict(base.determinism)["seed"] != dict(other.determinism)["seed"]
146	    assert "cfg" in dict(base.determinism)["input_hashes"]
147	
148	
149	# ---- primitives ----
150	
151	
152	def test_cosine_identical_orthogonal_opposite():
153	    assert cosine_similarity([1.0, 0.0], [2.0, 0.0]) == pytest.approx(1.0)
154	    assert cosine_similarity([1.0, 0.0], [0.0, 1.0]) == pytest.approx(0.0)
155	    assert cosine_similarity([1.0, 0.0], [-1.0, 0.0]) == pytest.approx(-1.0)
156	
157	
158	def test_cosine_zero_vector_is_zero_not_nan():
159	    sim = cosine_similarity([0.0, 0.0], [1.0, 1.0])
160	    assert sim == 0.0 and not math.isnan(sim)
161	
162	
163	def test_cosine_length_mismatch_raises():
164	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
165	        cosine_similarity([1.0], [1.0, 2.0])
166	
167	
168	def test_euclidean_distance():
169	    assert euclidean_distance([0.0, 0.0], [3.0, 4.0]) == pytest.approx(5.0)
170	
171	
172	def test_cosine_matrix_symmetric_unit_diagonal():
173	    m = cosine_similarity_matrix([[1.0, 0.0], [0.0, 1.0], [1.0, 1.0]])
174	    assert m[0][0] == pytest.approx(1.0)
175	    assert m[0][1] == pytest.approx(0.0)
176	    assert m[0][2] == pytest.approx(m[2][0])  # symmetric
177	
178	
179	def test_descriptive_stats():
180	    s = descriptive_stats([1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0])
181	    assert s["mean"] == pytest.approx(2.5)
182	    assert s["median"] == pytest.approx(2.5)
183	    assert s["min"] == 1.0 and s["max"] == 4.0
184	    assert s["std"] == pytest.approx(math.sqrt(5.0 / 3.0))  # sample std (ddof=1)
185	
186	
187	def test_descriptive_stats_single_value_zero_std():
188	    s = descriptive_stats([7.0])
189	    assert s["std"] == 0.0 and s["mean"] == 7.0

content.text: The test uses `match="extra_input 'cfg'"`. The `object()` has a non-reproducible repr (memory address), so `hash_value` raises TypeError. Let me confirm the discrimination claim: the `match` requires the exact string "extra_input 'cfg'". If the fix were reverted (raw exception propagated), the message wouldn't contain "extra_input 'cfg'", so it would fail. Let me verify by running the test and checking discrimination.

content.text: There's an unrelated diff in gates.py — let me check that determinism.py is clean (it is, not in the diff) and that gates.py diff is pre-existing, not from my work.

The gates.py diff cleared — that was a transient mid-restore snapshot artifact; working tree is now clean of my changes. determinism.py is correctly restored (not in diff). All 31 baseline tests pass.

Assessment:

Appropriateness: The fix (lines 139-146) wraps `hash_value(value)` in a try/except catching `(ValueError, TypeError)` and re-raises the same exception type with a message naming the offending key while chaining the original via `from exc`. This is correct and idiomatic: it preserves the exception type (so callers distinguishing ValueError/TypeError keep working), preserves the original cause, and adds the missing context. No regression — the reserved-key check (#B7) still precedes the loop, seed derivation is unaffected, and the extra-inputs-change-seed test still passes.

Completeness: The test discriminates. With the fix reverted, the test fails because the raw `hash_value` message ("cannot canonicalize a leaf of type builtins.'object'...") does not contain the `match` pattern "extra_input 'cfg'". With the fix in place it passes. Using `object()` is a valid trigger (non-reproducible repr → TypeError), and the test's `(ValueError, TypeError)` tuple covers both raise paths described in the fix comment.

input.reasoning: Fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py (lines 139-146): the extra_inputs loop now wraps hash_value(value) in try/except catching (ValueError, TypeError) and re-raises the SAME exception type with message f"extra_input {name!r} is not canonicalizable: {exc}" chained via 'from exc'. Appropriate: preserves exception type (callers distinguishing ValueError non-finite vs TypeError unreproducible-repr keep working), preserves original cause, adds the missing key context; idiomatic. No regression — the #B7 reserved 'corpus' key check still precedes the loop, seed derivation untouched, and test_build_artifact_extra_inputs_change_seed still passes. Complete + discriminating: test_build_artifact_names_the_bad_extra_input uses pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError), match="extra_input 'cfg'") with extra_inputs={"cfg": object()} (object() triggers TypeError via non-reproducible repr). I reverted the fix and the test FAILED ("Regex pattern did not match" — actual raw message "cannot canonicalize a leaf of type builtins.'object'..." lacks "extra_input 'cfg'"); restored fix and it PASSES. All 31 tests in tests/test_baseline.py pass. Working tree verified clean of my temporary revert.

content.text: Verified G-021. CONFIRMED — both appropriate and complete, with a discriminating regression test.
